desc:
The alcove at first looks small and cluttered but as your eyes scan the cavern
slowly you realise how deceptive it looks. The room is huge, but most of it is
covered by assorted furnaces, forges, iron rods, and the like. Suits of
completed armour line the far wall while bits and pieces seem to fly around the
floor. The workers pay you no mind as they go about their work, banging and
hammering, chipping and moulding.
name: Felak Ang-Gabil

desc:
All along this passage hang axes. From floor to ceiling, they hang majestically,
testament to times of war and glory long since passed from these proud people.
The axes are of all shapes and sizes, lengths and widths. You can spot legendary
axes, from tales told of heroes long gone, their only link back to the living
world. The axes line the way, separated only by white stones, carved bowl-like
into the wall, emitting a faint glow like muted candle-light into the hall.
Jutting out from the stone walls, two signs appear, one on the north wall and
one on the south, their lettering clear and dark in the stone.
name: Felak Baruk

desc:
This cavern is filled with instruments of death. Swords and axes line the wall,
maces and morningstars hang from bars running from the ceiling, while other
weapons of all sorts simply lie in heaps along the floor. The heat of the room
is oppressive, but the Dwarven smiths seem to pay it no mind. In fact, your
presence is either noted with a scowl or not noted at all. The smiths simply go
about their work, paying you no attention at all. A hastily scrawled note is
attached to the counter.
name: Felak Khazad-Baruk

desc:
Though from the outside this alcove looks simple, from within it grows to a
cavernous size, growing tall into the rock and furnished with many walkways and
terraces. Here, dwarves who have travelled from abroad may claim lodging for the
night, resting their backs on the gentle beds of the inn. The atmosphere is
relaxed, as if the tension from without cannot penetrate its hallowed stone
walls.
name: Khazad-dum Barazinkheled

desc:
All along this passage hang axes. From floor to ceiling, they hang majestically,
testament to times of war and glory long since passed from these proud people.
The axes are of all shapes and sizes, lengths and widths. You can spot legendary
axes, from tales told of heroes long gone, their only link back to the living
world. The axes line the way, separated only by white stones, carved bowl-like
into the wall, emitting a faint glow like muted candle-light into the hall.
name: Felak Baruk

desc:
This giant natural cavern spreads out before you like the workplace of Aule.
Stalactites hang down from the roof, bright gems glowing from their tips,
projecting a kalidescopic light all around you, flickering and dancing on the
walls. Giant stalagmites stand, carved masterfully into statues depicting Mahal
in different poses. Many dwarves are here at all times, resting, standing or
kneeling in prayer or reverence. An ornate altar stands here, dominating the
centre of the hall with its presence.
name: Felak Mahal

desc:
This small chamber slopes down into the mountain. Unlike the rest of the caverns
it is only dimly lit here; some few smoky torches serve to cast a flickering
light over the room. Sarcophagi line the floor. On the paths between them walk a
few Dwarves with bowed heads. Here they remember their loved ones, who walk no
more upon this earth, but sit with their fathers in the halls of waiting until
Mahal calls to them again.
name: The Hall of the Dead

desc:
Here sits the Dwarven king, King Under the Mountain, upon his throne of black
rock. His throne dominates the room, a large hall of black stone streaked with
gold. Suits of armour, shining softly in the light, line the walls and regal
carpets cover the floor. Many Dwarven courtiers move about, performing matters
for the king, who sits here listless, his face thrust deep in thought.
name: Felak Uzbad Khuzdul

desc:
This giant natural cavern spreads out before you like the workplace of Aule.
Stalactites hang down from the roof, bright gems glowing from their tips,
projecting a kaleidoscopic light all around you, flickering and dancing on the
walls. Giant stalagmites stand, carved masterfully into statues depicting Mahal
in different poses. Many dwarves are here at all times, resting, standing or
kneeling in prayer or reverence.
name: Felak Mahal

desc:
This small room is lined with shelves dug into the walls, and filled with
cabinets, desks, and chests filled with all sorts of great tomes, old and
yellowed, but still treasured for their store of knowledge. On every surface is
some scroll or map, wall-hanging or book. Here the records of the Dwarves and
their trials upon Arda are written and stored for future generations. Dwarves
linger here for many an hour, simply sitting and pondering, absorbing the past
as if to give them strength for the future. On a table sits a Khuzdul-Westron
dictionary. On a poster on the wall is the Song of Durin. There is also a family
tree of Durin's folk.
name: Felak Mazarbul

desc:
Light seems to shine from everywhere and nowhere here, illuminating the Dwarven
cavern in an eerie glow. Statues line the way, a testimony to greatness gone,
perhaps hinting at greatness to come. The walls are covered in tapestries of
semi-precious stones, forming rock paintings of Dwarven lords and heros, their
eyes shining rubies, looking at all those who pass beneath them. Their eyes seem
almost living, their glint at once sad and accusing, cold and caring, as if the
souls of the ancient Dwarves glimmered within them.
name: Felak Uzbadul

desc:
Light seems to shine from everywhere and nowhere here, illuminating the Dwarven
cavern in an eerie glow. Statues line the way, a testimony to greatness gone,
perhaps hinting at greatness to come. The walls are covered in tapestries of
semi-precious stones, forming rock paintings of Dwarven lords and heros, their
eyes shining rubies, looking at all those who pass beneath them. Their eyes seem
almost living, their glint at once sad and accusing, cold and caring, as if the
souls of the ancient Dwarves glimmered within them.
name: Felak Uzbadul

desc:
The great hall extends before you, an avenue lined by bright rubies, shining
like subterranean roses in the light. The floor is smooth, polished by the wise
and skilful hands of master Dwarven craftsmen. Carvings cover the walls,
bas-reliefs of ages long since past, heros long dead, and glory long since faded
from Durin's folk, remembered only in the scarlet glow of the rubies and the
stonework of Dwarven artists. The grim faces of the Dwarves around seem to burn
with determination. Perhaps the Hall of Rubies will ring again with the sounds
of Dwarven triumph. To the south, a sign of black rock, lettered in bright ruby
hangs above a lit alcove. From inside, the sounds of merriment can be heard.
name: Felak Barazinkheled

desc:
The great hall extends before you, an avenue lined by bright rubies, shining
like subterranean roses in the light. The floor is smooth, polished by the wise
and skilful hands of master Dwarven craftsmen. Carvings cover the walls,
bas-reliefs of ages long since past, heroes long dead, and glory long since
faded from Durin's folk, remembered only in the scarlet glow of the rubies and
the stonework of Dwarven artists. The grim faces of the Dwarves around seem to
burn with determination. Perhaps the Hall of Rubies will ring again with the
sounds of Dwarven triumph. To the south, the Hall of Dwarves can be seen, its
sombre walls rising straight up into the ever-dusk recesses of the chamber.
name: Felak Barazinkheled

desc:
The great hall continues before you, a dazzling crimson spectacle, lined by
bright rubies, shining like subterranean roses in the light. The floor is
smooth, polished by the wise and skilful hands of master Dwarven craftsmen. The
grim faces of the Dwarves around seem to burn with determination. Perhaps the
Hall of Rubies will ring again with the sounds of Dwarven triumph. All along
this majestic path lie benches carved from dark stone where Dwarves sit and
watch the world pass by the light of a thousand glittering eyes.
name: Felak Barazinkheled

desc:
The great hall extends before you, an avenue lined by bright rubies, shining
like subterranean roses in the light. The floor is smooth, polished by the wise
and skilful hands of master Dwarven craftsmen. Perhaps the Hall of Rubies will
ring again with the sounds of Dwarven triumph. Here the hall ends in a
carved-out alcove with benches smoothed out of the hewn rock walls. By the light
of the glittering gems, several Dwarves sit and read or just look around them in
awe at the work of their forefathers.
name: Felak Barazinkheled

desc:
The tavern is a high ceilinged room filled with the smoke of pipes and the
laughter of Dwarves at ease. Oval tables, growing out of the stone like
mushrooms, dot the floor, while a counter of pure marble marks the bar. Here, it
seems, all cares are forgotten, and the Dwarves who sit around you seem only
interested in a strong drink, a good meal, and the companionship of friends.
name: Khazad Kheled-Baruk

desc:
The vertical shaft embarks on a steep descent into the underground of the Blue
Mountains. Broad ladders have been fastened into the rocky walls by thick iron
eyebolts that grip around the legs. Far above, a wooden platform is barely
visible in the dim light where the ladders seem to end. The sound of mining
reaches up through the shaft echoing between the walls.
name: In a Mine Shaft

desc:
A large cavern hewn into the solid rock opens around a large shaft leading down
into the mountain. A sturdy wooden fence encircles the shaft to prevent
accidents. On the eastern side is an opening in the fence that leads onto a
wooden platform leaning over the shaft. The platform is used to haul ore out of
the mines with ropes; empty metal buckets used for this purpose have been
stacked in the southern end of the cavern. An opening in the northern wall leads
out of the mine area.
name: Entrance to the Mines

desc:
Through great labour a dome-shaped cavern has been excavated in the mountain.
The finely carved walls clearly show the remarkable skill given to the dwarves
by Aule. The dwarven guards have set a guard-post here, outside the mines to the
south. There is a table and four stools in the corner, a plank bed across the
floor, and in the centre is placed a fair-sized hearth generously warming the
cavern. To the south is a dark tunnel leading further into the mountain.
name: Barakun-dum

desc:
A great curved ceiling encloses a hall of remarkable workmanship. Runes and
strange sigils decorate the flawless walls. Tall, wide pillars spun with
glittering threads of metal stand at even intervals in two rows through the
hall. Through rectangular openings in the rock above the hall flows a calm
refreshing breeze carrying the cheerful sound of birds singing during the day.
While the hall is richly ornamented, it is apparently not used for the purpose
it was intended. At some time the southern wall was opened up, and that way now
lies a dark tunnel peering right into the heart of the mountain.
name: Gabil Tumundum

desc:
This wide way has been modelled as an avenue, in the style of Elves and Men. To
the sides are shops selling all sorts of goods, bustling with Dwarves and the
occasional Man or Elf. Between the shops bright outdoor scenes are painted, and
weary travellers may rest on benches along the way. On the ceiling high above
can be seen a bright blue sky dotted with puffy white clouds. Here the passage
bends slowly east and north. An arch in the southern wall leads into a large
cavern.
name: Trader's Way

desc:
This wide way has been modelled as an avenue, in the style of Elves and Men. To
the sides are shops selling all sorts of goods, bustling with Dwarves and the
occasional Man or Elf. Between the shops bright outdoor scenes are painted, and
weary travellers may rest on benches along the way. On the ceiling high above
can be seen a bright blue sky dotted with puffy white clouds.
name: Trader's Way

desc:
This wide way has been modelled as an avenue, in the style of Elves and Men. To
the sides are shops selling all sorts of goods, bustling with Dwarves and the
occasional Man or Elf. Between the shops bright outdoor scenes are painted and
weary travellers may rest on benches along the way. On the ceiling high above
can be seen a bright blue sky dotted with puffy white clouds. This part of the
way is particularly busy, as it intersects the main path from the outside to the
deeper parts of the Dwarven caves.
name: Trader's Way

desc:
This wide way has been modelled as an avenue, in the style of Elves and Men. To
the sides are shops selling all sorts of goods, bustling with Dwarves and the
occasional Man or Elf. Between the shops bright outdoor scenes are painted, and
weary travellers may rest on benches along the way. On the ceiling high above
can be seen a bright blue sky dotted with puffy white clouds. To the north lies
a prosperous-looking gem shop and to the west lies what looks like an inn.
name: Trader's Way

desc:
Dwarves are great lovers of precious metals and glittering gems, and their
jewellery crafting is unsurpassed in Middle-earth. Even now, in the long decline
of Durin's Folk, the finery on display is impressive. Though history has given
Dwarves and Elves few reasons to love one another, still Elves sometimes come
here to buy Dwarven handiwork.
name: Nordri's Treasure Chest

desc:
A largely ceremonial hall, it was the first mineshaft that led down to what is
now the second level of this Dwarven city. Dwarves hustle and bustle up and down
the great staircase at whose top you now stand. Guards stand watch by the
stairs, scowling at non-Dwarves, and a large sign is etched into the stone above
them. It has been a long time since the eyes of Men or Elves have been welcome
in the strongholds of the Dwarves, and this place is no exception.
name: Fortune's Delving

desc:
This passage leads east and west, delving its way through the mountains. This is
a residential area, and the walls are pocketed with rows of doors, one after the
other, each leading to an apartment where some solitary Dwarf resides. This area
is boring, almost drab. The grey stone walls continue their monotonous march
under the Ered Luin. There are no signs of decay or poor workmanship, but
neither is there any ornamentation or beauty in the design. The main passage
through this residential area turns east-to-south here, and a side passage
enters from the west. The sides of the passageways are marked by rows of doors,
one after the other, each leading to an apartment where some solitary Dwarf
resides. Simple, utilitarian construction has been used here. The grey stone
walls show no signs of decay or poor workmanship, but neither has much
ornamentation or beauty been incorporated in the design.
name: Dum Khazdul

desc:
You stand within an enormous cavern which extends beyond your vision and whose
ceiling is lost in darkness. Though the floor is perfectly smooth, the walls are
rough, filled with small niches and protrusions sticking into the hall. Arrayed
to your northwest are three tall, broad towers, cleverly crafted to look like
mountains. All three are made of hard grey stone, with snowy white peaks. The
sheer sides of the furthest gleam a dull red in the light.
name: Gabil Felak

desc:
You stand at the corner of an enormous cavern. It opens to the west and north,
and no end to its greatness can be seen. Just to the northwest there is a dark
pool. Towering over it on three sides are mountainous towers, grey and red and
snowy-capped. Fifty feet or so above you there is a large opening in the south
wall.
name: Gabil Felak

desc:
The ground slopes gently down to a wide clear pool. The area around you is
rough, except for a small flat surface near the pool, and several comfortable
paths leading away. Several Dwarves peer into the pool, thinking of the twilight
time when the first of their race awoke. They seem to be treating the water with
great reverence, taking care not to touch its surface.
name: Zaram Durinul

desc:
You are at the eastern edge of huge cavern deep within the earth. Just to your
west is a small pool, around which stand several unusually contemplative
Dwarves. Many other Dwarves walk quickly north or south.
name: Gabil Felak

desc:
To the east is the exit from the caves, but there is no hint here of sunlight.
Still, the high ceilings, cheery lighting, and fresh air make this place seem
almost like the outdoors. This seems to be something of a gathering place. There
are many stone benches and tables arranged together so that small groups may sit
and talk. Many Dwarves on their way in or out of the caves stop here to meet
with friends awhile, and in a few groups you also see an occasional Elf.
name: Entranceway

desc:
This wide way has been modelled as an avenue, in the style of Elves and Men. To
the sides are shops selling all sorts of goods, bustling with Dwarves and the
occasional Man or Elf. Between the shops bright outdoor scenes are painted, and
weary travellers may rest on benches along the way. On the ceiling high above
can be seen a bright blue sky dotted with puffy white clouds. A general store is
to the north.
name: Trader's Way

desc:
You are in a small, fragrant shop. All around you is the aroma of dried
pipeweed. The source of the smell is obvious; pipeweed leaves and plants are
hanging from strings and racks all over the shop. There is also a small case
here displaying pipes and other objects. Small barrels are stacked around the
side walls. At the back of the shop there is a small desk and a chair. To the
west you can return to the main thoroughfare.
name: Felak-Narbulum

desc:
You stand at the very entrance of the Dwarven stronghold deep within the Ered
Luin. To the east stand two huge iron doors. In times of peace they stand open,
but should the warning horns blow they can be quickly closed and barred, and
provide a formidable defence. The hall rises high above your head, its long
lines drawing your eyes to the wide space above you. Far from a damp and dreary
cave, this is a mansion - a grand hallway wrought of rich stone. Carved out of
the living rock of its walls, busts of the great Dwarven kings stare into the
hall. You can make out Dain I and Thror, watching over the entrance to the
kingdom of the longbeards.
name: Entrance Hall

desc:
Here are the simple barracks of the Dwarven home-guard. Wooden beds are bunked
along the walls and a weapons-rack rests by the door. The home-guard is sworn to
protect the city from all invaders, and their loyalty and ferocity have earned
them much renown.
name: Guard Barracks

desc:
This dark stone cell shows little of the care that has been put into the
stonework throughout the rest of the caves. Alongside one wall is a low stone
bench, and on the other are several cots. Beyond that there is nothing but
smooth, impenetrable grey walls.
name: Stone Cell

desc:
Here are the simple barracks of the Dwarven home-guard. Wooden beds are bunked
along the walls and a weapons-rack rests by the door. The home-guard is sworn to
protect the city from all invaders, and their loyalty and ferocity have earned
them much renown.
name: Guard Barracks

desc:
This wide way has been modelled as an avenue, in the style of Elves and Men. To
the sides are shops selling all sorts of goods, bustling with Dwarves and the
occasional Man or Elf. Between the shops bright outdoor scenes are painted, and
weary travellers may rest on benches along the way. On the ceiling high above
can be seen a bright blue sky dotted with puffy white clouds. From the north
come smells of baking. To the east Trader's Way opens to the outside.
name: Trader's Way

desc:
The Dwarves make bread in their own image, short and hard. You see many Dwarves
filter in and out of the bakery, chomping hardily on bread that looks like
stone. From the look of the baker's wares, he very well may forge them rather
than baking them in an oven.
name: Frar's Bread Forge

desc:
The floor of this rather spartan room is covered in hay. A pungent, earthy smell
fills the area. Various squeaks and growls can be heard from the cages and pens
lining the wall. A battered wooden table stands in the middle of the room.
name: Animal Trainer's

desc:
This pond seems to have an underground source, as there is no visible inlet
stream. It is not huge, just big enough for children to play in the water, and
some animals to drink from. You could take a boat out on it, but you could not
go very far at all.
name: A Big Pond

desc:
The track you were on takes a turn for the better, as the loose gravel and rocks
give way to tight fitting stones, inlaid with polished ore of various colours.
The walkway ends in front of a smooth wall. It is so sharply defined that it
might be a gate, but you see no breaks in it at all.
name: An Inlaid Stone Walkway

desc:
A very large stairway, carved from marble, leads up towards the surface and down
into the Dwarven Halls in the heart of the Blue Mountains. The massive steps are
flanked by a smooth paved road, which the Dwarves use to transport heavy items
to and from their halls. The walls are covered with Dwarven runes and with
carvings of important events of the history of the people of Durin. Torches are
set in the walls and light the stairway efficiently; a light breeze from below
keeps the air clear and breathable.
name: Marble Staircase

desc:
A worn trail ends here, at the foot of a high stone tower. An ornate archway to
the east leads inside. To the north, beyond a large wooden building, lies the
bank of the Nan Duin, while the trail continues to the south. To the west, a
wide crack in a hill attracts your attention. The inside of the crack is
lamplit, and you can see a very large marble staircase leading down.
name: End of the Worn Trail

desc:
You have arrived at a T junction. To the north the trail ends at the foot of a
stone tower, near the Nan Duin. To the east you can see a dense forest in the
distance, while to the south the winding trail climbs to the Blue Mountains,
flanking the west side of the forest.
name: Branch in the Worn Trail

desc:
The trail runs east and west through some low, rocky hills. Not far to the north
a stone tower attracts your curiosity. It is almost certainly inhabited, as you
can guess from the smoke coming out of the chimney. The glittering waters of the
Nan Duin flow from the west to the east behind the tower, mirroring the
mountaintops. It is a fascinating sight.
name: Worn Trail

desc:
The trail descends from the foothills to the west and plunges itself into the
heart of a large forest to the south. North of here, the Nan Duin flows calmly
to the east; you can follow its southern bank to the east, into the outskirts of
the forest. Not far to the northwest, you can see a high stone tower.
name: Worn Trail

desc:
The trail turns east into the heart of a dense forest; to the north the trees
become sparser, and an area of rocky foothills begins. The woods to the
southwest are unusually calm, while to the northwest a stone tower dominates the
landscape.
name: Worn Trail

desc:
The trail cuts the dense forest in a more or less east-west direction. On both
sides, huge trees and tangled weeds flank the path, and prevent you from leaving
it. Far to the northwest, the forest seems to grow thinner; right against the
horizon, you barely manage to see the profile of a high tower.
name: Worn Trail

desc:
You continue your journey along a trail in the middle of a dense forest. A
nearly continuous wall of trees and greenery blocks your way north and east.
Behind the thick trees, something seems to be growling at you. You feel that the
ferocious creatures of the wood want to drink your blood.
name: Worn Trail

desc:
The trail winds its way through a dense forest, turning to the north and east. A
smaller trail leads west into the depths of the wood. On the trunk of a massive
tree to the south, you can see the marks of many mighty axe blows. Some pieces
of a fragmented skeleton lie at the foot of the tree. You can only try to guess
what happened... Far to the northwest, you can barely see some smoke rising to
the sky.
name: Worn Trail

desc:
The trail stretches far away in an east-west direction, climbing gently to the
west. A forest of huge trees flanks it on both sides. Miles behind the treetops,
you can spot the snow capped peaks of the Blue Mountains.
name: Worn Trail

desc:
The trail winds its way to the west and south through a dense forest. In this
place, a hole in the otherwise impenetrable barrier of entangled greenery could
allow you to enter the thick woods to the north, but something - perhaps the
occasional high pitched howls and deep growls - might discourage the
faint-hearted from venturing among the ancient trees.
name: Worn Trail

desc:
You are on a dusty trail leading north and east through the woods. Some
branches, which evidently obstructed passage, have been cut and now lay on the
border of the trail. To the west, a seldom used path enters the forest. You can
smell the presence of mushrooms in that direction, though you cannot figure out
the species.
name: Worn Trail

desc:
Huge trees, which seem to reach the sky, flank the trail on both sides here. You
would rest for a while in their shadow, but your instinct tells you that danger
awaits you in the forest. The trail turns to the west and south.
name: Worn Trail

desc:
The trail winds its way north and east through the Blue Mountains. A small path,
almost completely hidden by high grass, climbs the steep mountain side to the
south. To the north a dense, gloomy forest flanks the trail. A thick wall of
huge trees and entwined greenery prevents you from entering the woods here.
name: Worn Trail

desc:
Maybe you should remain on this path as opposed to entering the forest to your
north. Uncertainty settles on you at the thought of entering. Can you handle the
threats and dangers of the dark gloomy place? Many glowing eyes darting in the
blackness peer at you, and almost lure you in with their tempting gleam. The
path continues to the south and west.
name: Worn Trail

desc:
The trail continues to the north and east here. The presence of life is sparse,
limited mostly to animals and birds. The treetops are alive with the songs of
swallows and finch, and the grounds are occupied by squirrels, fox, and deer.
The river provides a good source of water, and food from bushes is plentiful.
Habitation by more humanoid creatures is not in evidence, except for the trail.
name: Worn Trail

desc:
The trees beside the trail are tall and their leaves block out the light from
the sun and the moon. The forest continues to get thicker to the south and west.
But to the north the trees thin out and dense vegetation covers the ground. A
vast plain stretches far to the east.
name: Trail Entering a Forest

desc:
Although you feel safe on the path you are on, there is no saying what dwells
within these dark woods. The beech grove to your south does not seem too
threatening, but curious, some would say frightening, sounds can be heard from
the thicket of ash trees north of here.
name: Forest Trail

desc:
Here, as the path bends south and east, a tiny track strays from it, heading its
own way through grassy slopes to the west. To the north the ground slants slowly
upward into the forest, and from it you can from time to time hear the forest
birds stir up a noisy symphony.
name: Forest Trail

desc:
The forest path steers steadily on north and south between the trees. You may
enter a grove of beeches to the east, but west the overlapping branches
stubbornly denies you access. The gap in the forest seems to grow wider within
sight to the north.
name: Forest Trail

desc:
You stand on a modest forest path, the horizon on all sides veiled by a mist of
trees. The forest seems less dense to the east, tall and bulky trees which from
here look like beeches. Westward you can enter a more intimidating part of the
forest, dense woods which seem to have the very darkness of the Pits of Utumno
nestled within. To stay out of the forest, you can follow the path trailing
north and south.
name: Forest Trail

desc:
The river flows gently to the southeast in its valley across the Blue Mountains.
In the fresh, glittering water some fish dart here and there, seeking food or
swimming to the far Sea or to the mountains. To the northeast, from the top of a
cliff, a small stream flows into the river with a waterfall. On sunny days, the
rainbows in the waterfall are a breathtaking sight.
name: The Nan Duin

desc:
SPLASH! As soon as you transit below the high cliff to the north, the water from
a stream that ends in a waterfall right above soaks you completely. The water is
cool, and, as soon as you recover your breath, you begin to find the experience
quite pleasant. Just remember to move away before you catch a cold!
name: In the Waterfall

desc:
The river stretches far to the east, until it vanishes beyond the horizon. Far
to the southeast you begin to see a high tower, from which a whiffle of smoke
rises into the sky. To the south the river is flanked by a rough marble cliff.
Some holes in the cliff look like dwarven mines. From the north you can barely
hear the noise of a waterfall. It must be either small, or very far.
name: The Nan Duin

desc:
The Nan Duin stretches far to the west and east in its course across the Blue
Mountains. The northern bank of the river is barren, totally devoid of
vegetation. To the south, a large wooden building and a tower lie. However, the
southern bank is too steep to leave the river and investigate.
name: The Nan Duin

desc:
The river flows calmly from the west to the east. To the north, a cliff prevents
you from leaving the water, while to the south you see a high tower. From the
top of the tower one can see for many miles around.
name: The Nan Duin

desc:
The Nan Duin flows calmly from the west to the southeast, as it descends from
the Blue Mountains to the Narbrethil Forest. Not far to the southwest, a stone
tower attracts your attention.
name: The Nan Duin

desc:
The river here turns slightly from the northwest to the east, where it enters a
large forest. To the south a trail ends at the bank of the river, while a few
hundred meters west of here you see a high tower which dominates the entire
landscape.
name: The Nan Duin

desc:
On its course from the Blue Mountains to the Gulf of Lhun, the Nan Duin enters
the dense Narbrethil Forest in this place. Far to the west, you can spot a tall
tower, from which a whiff of smoke rises into the sky.
name: The Nan Duin

desc:
On both banks of the river you can see the huge trees of the Narbrethil Forest.
Some fish are swimming in the water, and you can hear birds chirping on the
northern bank. Not far to the west the forest comes to an end, and trails appear
on both sides of the river.
name: The Nan Duin

desc:
The river stretches from the west to the east in the middle of the Narbrethil
Forest. Far to the west, the forest seems to grow thinner. The thick undergrowth
prevents you from entering the dark woods to the south, from which some distant
howls spoil the peaceful feeling this place would give you otherwise.
name: The Nan Duin

desc:
After flowing for a long time in a more or less west to east direction, the
river here turns to the southeast. A forest of great trees grows on both sides
of the river. To the east, a nearly invisible path leads inside the forest.
name: The Nan Duin

desc:
East and north of here, the Nan Duin flows in its valley across the Blue
Mountains and down to the Grey Havens. To the southwest, the forest of
Narbrethil has an intimidating look, and you feel that some howling, evil
creature has made its home there.
name: The Nan Duin

desc:
The river flows from the west to the south, across a forest. The water is so
clear that you can watch the fish swimming between the rocks on the bottom of
the river.
name: The Nan Duin

desc:
The river flows from the north to the east, crossing a dense forest. To the
south and west, the thick undergrowth stops you from leaving the river. You can
see a dock not far to the east.
name: The Nan Duin

desc:
The Nan Duin stretches far to the east and west through the Blue Mountains. As
it flows, the river seems to emit faint sounds, as if it was humming the tunes
of miners, like the Dwarves long ago. You are in the heart of Dwarf country, and
you suspect the remembrances of history are affecting you. Will you meet any of
the sturdy folk in your expeditions? The river goes to the east and west here,
or you may slide up to a dock just north of here.
name: The Nan Duin

desc:
The old trees grow very dense here, the crowns almost blocking out all light
from above. The underbrush is sparse and consists mainly of leaves, broken
branches and dead fallen trees. Small game trails can be seen darting between
the many trees. The forest continues all around, however, the trees seem to grow
closer together to the west.
name: Ancient Forest

desc:
You stand in a mixed grove of chestnut and oak trees. A thin, whispering sound
fills your ears, apparently just the wind stirring the upper twigs and branches
of these tall beeches. The grove extends east and north, while a path can be
seen curving around it to the south and west.
name: Whispering Woods

desc:
You stand in the northern part of a grove of beeches, tall oak trees with
thick-set trunks and boughs with a multitude of branches. The undergrowth
partially covers the many interlacing roots, making it easy to trip and fall if
you are unwary. You spy a path though the trees to your north and west.
name: Whispering Woods

desc:
The limbs of the giant oak trees interlace above, forming a canopy. Little
underbrush manages to grow here, as the canopy of leaves blocks most of the
sunlight. The ground is thickly covered with fallen leaves and an almost
constant rustling fills the air as the forest wildlife goes about its business.
The woods continue to the west and east but a trail lies to the north. Southward
lies the oldest part of the forest.
name: Under the Forest Eaves

desc:
Rough, grassy hills fill the landscape in all directions. On the ground, more or
less evenly distanced, you can see the entrances of a mole colony. From time to
time some of the small animals poke their snout out of a hole, but immediately
retreat as they perceive your presence.
name: Foothills

desc:
Your journey has brought you to an area of rough, grassy hills. The wind blows
through many small valleys, making strange whistling sounds. Not far to the
north, you can see a large marble cliff, which seems to have been extensively
mined by the Dwarves. A whiff of smoke rises into the sky from behind a hill to
the northeast.
name: Foothills

desc:
The trail winds its way up into the Blue Mountains to the southwest, and
descends into the valley of the Nan Duin east. Many rough foothills, which have
been extensively mined by the Dwarves, surround you and block your vision of the
horizon, except to the northeast, where, in the distance, you can see an
isolated tower.
name: Worn Trail

desc:
Here the trail, which runs between some foothills and a dark forest, turns north
and west. Far to the north you can see a tower. The tangled greenery prevents
you from entering the fear-inspiring woods to the south-east. As you try to peek
inside the darkness, you suddenly see a glowing red pair of eyes. Then it is
gone... or was it your imagination?
name: Worn Trail

desc:
You are travelling through an area of rough, grassy hills at the feet of the
Blue Mountains. The hills become higher to the west, while to the east the
ground gently slopes down to a dense forest. You can barely see a worn, winding
trail following the edge of the wood, and you wonder where it would lead you if
you allowed your steps to follow it. A mine shaft descends into the darkness at
your feet. You hear the noise of the Dwarven miners working below.
name: Foothills

desc:
The trail countinues its long journey across the Blue Mountains to the south and
to the east. The foothills to the north show extensive signs of mining
operations. The Dwarves have dwelt in this region for thousands of years.
name: Worn Trail

desc:
The trail runs near the edge of a dark forest. To the south the huge trees
become less dense, and you can leave the hills to enter the wood. North and west
the trail continues its long journey across the Nan Duin valley. Far to the
northeast, a whiff of smoke rises into the sky.
name: Worn Trail

desc:
The huge trees here grow so thick that no light at all reaches the ground.
Ominous howls echo through the woods, filling your heart with despair. You feel
that some large, predatory beast wants to feed upon you. Here and there patches
of grey, dead-smelling moss grow on the tree trunks. Warped, pale, almost
skeletal bushes make your movement difficult.
name: Dark Forest

desc:
The huge trees here grow so thick that no light at all reaches the ground.
Ominous howls echo through the woods, filling your heart with despair. You feel
that some large, predatory beast wants to feed upon you. Here and there patches
of grey, dead-smelling moss grow on the tree trunks. Warped, pale, almost
skeletal bushes make your movement difficult.
name: Dark Forest

desc:
You find yourself at the edge of a large pine forest. The trees are very large,
but in this place they are distanced enough to allow you to see the sky above
you. Not far to the south, however, the forest becomes extremely thick and dark.
Red eyes blink in the darkness, spying each move you make. To the east you can
barely see a trail crossing the forest.
name: Light Forest

desc:
Small dried fallen branches and pine needles crackle as you travel through this
dense forest of huge trees. Above you, a few squirrels jump from one tree to the
next. You realise their presence more by hearing than by sight, as the branches
are so entwined that nearly no light filters through them. To the west the
forest becomes even more dense and gloomy.
name: Dense Forest

desc:
You have reached a small clearing in the Narbrethil Forest. Huge pine trees
surround you in all directions. As you look west and south, however, you realise
that the forest is much thicker than to the east.
name: Light Forest

desc:
Small dried fallen branches and pine needles crackle as you travel through this
dense forest of huge trees. Above you, a few squirrels jump from one tree to the
next. You realise their presence more by hearing than by sight, as the branches
are so entwined that nearly no light filters through them. To the north the
forest becomes even more dense and gloomy.
name: Dense Forest

desc:
You find yourself on the western corner of the huge Narbrethil Forest. Large
pine trees grow here and there, and become thicker and thicker as you travel to
the east. North and west of here, a worn trail flanks the forest and leads
towards the Blue Mountains to the south. Some distant howls remind you that this
is not a safe place to rest your tired bones.
name: Light Forest

desc:
The trail climbs the steep slopes of the Blue Mountains to the west, and
descends rapidly to the Nan Duin Valley to the north. To the east there is a
light forest, while to the south you can see deer grazing in a field full of
grass. Some tall pine trees grow in a double line along the trail.
name: Worn Trail

desc:
A light, cool breeze descending from the Blue Mountains carries to your nostrils
the scent of delicate flowers. Here and there you can see some deer grazing on
the short grass, and a nightingale is singing from the branches of an isolated
fir tree. You relax for a moment in this calm place, before climbing the
mountains to the south or entering a long winding trail not far to the north.
name: Grassy Plain

desc:
The plains begin to slope upwards to the south, where the Blue Mountains rise
high towards the sky. A low wall of stones has been built to the east; beyond
the wall the grass grows very high. To the northeast, behind the grass, you can
spot a dark and forbidding forest of pine trees.
name: Grassy Plain

desc:
You force your way through a sea of high grass, being careful not to put your
foot inside one of the numerous rabbit holes that dot the ground. Some small
animals run quickly among the grass, making a rustling noise. To the south the
ground rises quickly towards the Blue Mountains, while to the northeast you can
see a large, dense forest of pine trees.
name: In the High Grass

desc:
You force your way through a sea of high grass, being careful not to put your
foot inside one of the numerous rabbit holes that dot the ground. Some small
animals run quickly among the grass, making a rustling noise. To the south the
ground rises gently towards a small valley in the Blue Mountains, while to the
northeast you can see a large, dense forest of pine trees. The grass becomes
shorter to the east.
name: In the High Grass

desc:
You find yourself in a dense forest of huge pine trees. The thick undergrowth
hinders your movements, and the entwined branches above you cover nearly all the
sky, making this a rather gloomy place. From time to time, a series of deep
growls and excited howls echoes through the woods. You are not sure you wish to
meet the source of the noises.
name: Dense Forest

desc:
You have reached the southern edge of a large pine forest. The trees are very
high; squirrels jump among their branches, and hide as soon as they perceive
your presence. To the west there is a field filled with very high grass; to the
south, a barely visible path climbs the mountain side, bending and twisting to
take advantage of the few places where the ascent is not too steep. Even so, you
think it would be better to rest for a while before attempting to climb the
path.
name: Edge of a Forest

desc:
In this section of Narbrethil Forest, the huge pine trees grow far from each
other, so that during daytime the light of the sun reaches the ground. Many
bushes grow among the trees; a small, nearly invisible path leads east to west.
The trees become much thicker to the north, where a barrier of living wood
prevents you from reaching the heart of the forest, and to the west.
name: Light Forest

desc:
In this section of Narbrethil Forest, the huge pine trees grow far from each
other, so that during daytime the light of the sun reaches the ground. Many
bushes grow among the trees; a small, nearly invisible path leads east to west.
The trees become much thicker to the north, where a barrier of living wood
prevents you from reaching the heart of the forest.
name: Light Forest

desc:
In this section of Narbrethil Forest, the huge pine trees grow far from each
other, so that during daytime the light of the sun reaches the ground. Many
bushes grow among the trees; a small, nearly invisible path leads east to west.
The trees become much thicker to the north, where a barrier of living wood
prevents you from reaching the heart of the forest.
name: Light Forest

desc:
In this section of Narbrethil Forest, the huge pine trees grow far from each
other, so that during daytime the light of the sun reaches the ground. Many
bushes grow among the trees; a small, nearly invisible path leads east to west.
The trees become much thicker to the north, where a barrier of living wood
prevents you from reaching the heart of the forest, and to the east.
name: Light Forest

desc:
You find yourself in a dense forest of huge pine trees. The thick undergrowth
hinders your movements, and the entwined branches above you cover nearly all the
sky, making this a rather gloomy place. A light breeze carries the scent of
mushrooms from the north.
name: Dense Forest

desc:
This section of Narbrethil Forest is filled with mushrooms. Mushrooms of all
sizes and descriptions grow everywhere: bright red or dull brown on the ground,
small yellow-green mushrooms on the trunks and on the branches of the huge pine
trees. Unfortunately, many of the fungi are poisonous. To the north and east a
trail flanks the forest, while to the south the trees become thicker.
name: Light Forest

desc:
A large pine forest grows northwest of here. The trees are not very thick, so
that you can clearly see the sky, and amid the undergrowth you recognise many
strawberry bushes. To the south the trees gradually become even less dense,
until the forest gives way to the grassy slopes of the Blue Mountains.
name: Light Forest

desc:
The ground here slopes steeply, forcing you to zigzag if you want to climb up to
the mountains or down to the bottom of the Nan Duin Valley, where a grassy field
flanks a large pine forest. You take a deep breath and continue your long
journey.
name: Grassy Slope

desc:
You find yourself on the slopes of the Blue Mountains, south of the valley of
the Nan Duin. A small twisting path leads east through the mountains, and north
towards a large pine forest. To the west the ground slopes down to a grassy
plain, while to the south you can see the entrance of a Dwarven mine. It seems
to have been abandoned: you can see no miners around, and a deadly silence fills
the place.
name: Blue Mountains

desc:
The snow-capped peaks of the Blue Mountains surround you on all sides, except to
the north, where a small, nearly invisible path leads towards a large pine
forest. The flanks of the mountain are covered with grass and small bushes; some
goats are grazing nearby.
name: Blue Mountains

desc:
You are at the entrance of a long tunnel, supported by strong-looking timbers of
typical dwarven make. You immediately notice that something is amiss: the
timbers and the granite walls bear black scorches, as if a searing flame had
touched them for an instant. In one place the timbers have been so severely
damaged that part of the ceiling has collapsed. On the ground, at your feet,
lies the charred skeleton of a Dwarf. The tunnel continues to the east.
name: Abandoned Dwarven Mine

desc:
You have reached the end of the abandoned mine. To the west, the tunnel climbs
up towards the surface. Some fire-consumed mining gear lies here. As you inspect
the walls of the tunnel, you realise that the iron ore vein which attracted the
Dwarves to this place is far from being exhausted. The black scorches on the
timbers are not likely to have been caused by an accident, otherwise the
stubborn Dwarves would have returned.
name: Abandoned Dwarven Mine

desc:
Layers of lively grass fill out the landscape. A group of deer carefully meander
along, chewing on the grass. Squirrels run up and down a tree branch playfully.
You take a stretch and enjoy the beauty nature offers you. The grass grows tall
and unhindered, as the breeze blows past, the stalks brush against each other in
a symphony of rustling sounds. A trail is clearly marked to the north, while to
the east there is more grass.
name: A Grassy Field

desc:
Short grass and colorful flowers grow all around you and make you feel at ease.
You stop for a moment and relax, watching the beautiful butterflies flying
between the flowers and some bouncing rabbits chewing the grass. Not far to the
north you can see a large forest, while the mountains to the south are too steep
to climb.
name: Grassy Field

desc:
Short grass and colorful flowers grow all around you and make you feel at ease.
You stop for a moment and relax, watching the beautiful butterflies flying
between the flowers and some fat rabbits chewing the grass. Not far to the north
you can see a large forest, while the mountains to the south are too steep to
climb.
name: Grassy Field

desc:
You have reached the southern edge of a large pine forest. The trees are very
high; squirrels jump among their branches, and hide as soon as they perceive
your presence. The smell of resin and of wild berries fills the air. South of
you the Blue Mountains ascend steeply; you stop for a while, contemplating their
snow covered peaks.
name: Edge of a Forest

desc:
The cave is dimly lit with an eerie yellow-green light emitted by glowing mosses
growing on the walls. Huge stalagmites rise from the cavern floor, forming a
maze of pillars. Following the sound of dripping water, you find a large, dark
pool beneath a cluster of lichen-encrusted stalactites.
name: Gloomy Cave

desc:
You are squatting inside a small space which seems to be used as a storage
chamber for a hunter. It might even be used as a hideaway, if such a thing
should be needed in this quiet region. Pelts and hides from lions, wolves and
bears lie stacked innermost, and some forestry and hunting equipment is heaped
by your feet.
name: Inside a Tree

desc:
In the midst of the grove stands an old and crooked rowan, shedding its leaves
into a small pond by its feet. Also by the pond lies some crude, chalk-white
slates covered with golden hair, the hair of mountain lions. Spread out around a
huge uprooted stump to the east, is a circle of blue violets.
name: Grove of Rowans

desc:
You are walking on the treshold of a green grove of rowan trees. A rocky ridge
borders it to the south and east, and north of it lies a tall, bracken-covered
hill. From here you can follow a valley westwards or move to the centre of the
grove to the east.
name: Outside a Grove

desc:
This valley floor is draped with dense green shrubbery and sprinkled with a few
thickets of maple shrubs. A path is swallowed by the shrubbery as it dives into
it from the west. Following the valley east, you will reach a solitary grove of
trees protected by the embrace of the ridge to the south.
name: Between Hills

desc:
You are on a faint path which here rounds the western edge of a jagged ridge.
There is valley not far below you to the west, but far enough to make your fall
a lethal one, should you get the notion to try descent. The path fades away as
it cuts in between the ridge and hills eastwards.
name: A Gentle Rise

desc:
Someone has recently moved into this cave. The walls have been carven with a
much more careful hand than the water that eroded this cavern long ago. The
walls are bare of dirt and fungus, except for one corner in which several large,
and apparently edible, mushrooms grow. Various shelves and drawers and chairs,
all made skillfully from wood, adorn this chamber. On the ground, next to a bed
of moss, lies a very large bear-skin carpet.
name: Inhabited Cave

desc:
Moss covers the ground at your feet, and it grows thicker towards the entrance
of a cave to the east. In this moss you see some heavy footprints, leading to a
door of pine. North are the outskirts of a pine-forest.
name: Blue Mountains

desc:
Your feet make crispy noises treading the brittle undergrowth in this narrow
vale. Steep hillsides covered by moss and vines leads you in a north-south
direction, and the convex shapes of hills to your north block all view of what
might lie beyond. To the south the valley leads out into a light forest.
name: Shadowed Vale

desc:
A narrow track leads through this uniform forest of birches. To the north, where
the ground has an upward-sloping tendency, there is a thinning out of the black
and white trunks. West of here a tangled web of shrubs blocks the way. You might
follow the path north or south from here, or force your way through the dense
foliage to the east.
name: Birchwood

desc:
Here stand slender, young birches, lesser trees which one day will grow as tall
as those in the more robust forest east and south. Northward, the forest bows to
tall hills through which a narrow vale cuts like a gaping wound. The opposite
way lies a forest clearing, obviously man-made.
name: Young Birchwood

desc:
You are on the eastern fringe of a grassy field. Here trees surround you to your
south and east. Tall birches stand as pale sentinels on both sides of a carved,
wooden arch which marks the beginning of a track leading south into the forest.
name: Grazing Fields

desc:
A rough path leads you around the snow-capped peaks of the Blue Mountains, and
west into a small valley. The ground is nearly barren: only a few patches of
grass and some small bushes grow here and there.
name: Blue Mountains

desc:
You are at the bottom of a small grassy valley in the Blue Mountains. A narrow
path, which has nearly been hidden by the vegetation, leads to the mountains
around you. As you gaze around, you see that to the north, where the ground
descends gently, the grass becomes very high; beyond the high grass there is a
large pine forest. To the south, after a steep ascent, lies a small gap in the
mountains.
name: Valley in the Blue Mountains

desc:
Mountainous hills surround you, making the uneven track you are following
invaluable in keeping a steady direction. The track crawls steadily on in in a
north-south direction, with any sight of what lies ahead obscured by the wayside
slopes.
name: An Animal Track

desc:
Looking out across this peaceful farmland, you see herds of sheep grazing
quietly and untended. A high fence runs east-west a small march to your north.
You can also spot a small track between the hills in that direction.
name: Grazing Fields

desc:
A rough path leads you around the snow-capped peaks of the Blue Mountains, and
east into a small valley. The ground is nearly barren: only a few patches of
grass and some small bushes grow here and there.
name: Blue Mountains

desc:
A small path winds its way through the snow-capped peaks of the Blue Mountains.
This place is nearly barren; only a few small patches of grass grow among the
rocks. A high pitched whistle breaks the silence when you reach a turn in the
path. Perhaps it is a marmot, fearing for the safety of its home.
name: Blue Mountains

desc:
These sloping hills are covered with fences, probably used by farmers as pasture
during summer. North of you the Blue Mountains rise above the hills, where
dwarves are rumoured to have built a city.
name: Grassy Hillside

desc:
Here lies a stretch of farmland wedged between the hills of the Blue Mountains.
A high fence can be seen a couple of stone throws away in all directions except
east, where more fields lie.
name: Grazing Fields

desc:
Apparently these fields are being used for grazing a local farmer's livestock, a
small herd of sheep. A stout fence has been built to the north side of the
pasture, perhaps to protect the animals from the large carnivores, which must
live in the mountainous country beyond.
name: Grazing Fields

desc:
You are on the southern boundary of a wide field of grass. A fence blocks the
way west of you, but a slim gate, only wide enough for a man to pass through,
has been put into the fence just here. Several oak branches are used to block
the gate, and inspecting it closer, you see obvious strain in both the branches
and the gate. A string of feathers, animal bones and bits of furs hang on top of
the gate, out of your reach.
name: Grazing Fields

desc:
You are on a path which doesn't seem to be much in use. Southwards it leads into
the hills of the Blue Mountains, while east it dissolves more or less at a
solid-looking gate which doesn't seem openable from this side. A grisly wolf
skull has been pinned to the top of the gate.
name: Path in the Hills

desc:
You stand on a hillside some way above a small path which is twisting and
twining its way through the uneven terrain. Your feet wade through rugged brush,
making sounds like the crackle of a cheery campfire. You can re-enter the path
south or west, depending on which way you are bound.
name: Off the Track

desc:
The path winds through these morose hills spellbound by the shadows of huge
mountains in the west. It is possible to ascend some way up in the hillsides to
the east, or you may elect to follow the path north or south.
name: Path in the Hills

desc:
You tread soft grass, seeing nothing but fields to your north and west.
Likewise, east and south is nothing but a dense wall of trees. Gaining access to
the forest from this spot is seemingly impossible, but you think you can spot a
break in the forest a little further northeast.
name: Grazing Fields

desc:
You stand on a small avenue which has been cleared through a forest of tall
birches. The avenue leads out of the gloomy forest light to you north, through a
masterfully carved wooden arch. Going forth south into deeper forest will lead
you who knows where, but the makers of this avenue must obviously be civilised
creatures.
name: Track through a Birchwood

desc:
You stand in a field of tree trunks, some of them uprooted. Thick logs lie
stacked in a pile, the stack being held in place by beams driven into the ground
at the base of it. Movement north and south are your only alternatives, the
forest in other directions being too dense.
name: Among Logs and Trunks

desc:
The track ends on the threshold of a lone cabin. The small abode is completely
surrounded by the forest on all sides except where the track you are on comes to
meet it. The green door, inset with a tiny window not much bigger than a
peephole, is standing half ajar, welcoming you to step inside.
name: In Front of a Cabin

desc:
This is a cosy living room, readily revealing the occupation of its inhabi-
tants. In a corner there stands a spinning wheel and a stool, and on the floor
beside the wheel lies a bundle of the finest wool. Different-colour yarns are
stacked in another corner, above which hangs a shelf with cans containing
strange substances. Among the other furniture you notice a chest containing wool
sweaters and other knitwear. Oddly enough, the only window in this room is a big
glass window put between two of the ceiling support beams, providing rich light
on a sunny day.
name: Shepherdess' Home

desc:
This is a modest room, divided into two parts, kitchen and bedchamber. By the
kitchen table stands a cabinet filled with various plates and cutlery. A bench
and stove for food-preparation are also present. Should you feel tired, lay down
and relax on the double bed at the other end of the room, and there is also a
bathtub in one corner, which you could use to get that black grime off your
body.
name: Homely Kitchen

desc:
The path climbs up to the top of a bluff hillside. Turning north, you see fields
dotted white with flocks of what must be sheep. Eastward a forest rests its huge
bulk, continuing almost out of eyesight. The path meanders west into the hills,
and south it crosses a small valley stream on its way to the top of a rotund
hill.
name: Path above a Forest

desc:
You walk on a small path between dreary hills, looking intently for life
somewhere in the rippling landscape. Far away, you can hear a dulin sing, and
unseen creatures scuttle through the dense undergrowth. East and west the path
leads on.
name: Path in the Hills

desc:
At this point the craggy slopes to your west force the path to turn north and
east. A scree has dispatched a jumble of rocks onto the path, making riding
impossible. Below a small bush wedged between rocks to your west, you notice a
faint discolouration on the stones.
name: Path in the Hills

desc:
You are following a rarely used path climbing up to a pale hill in the east.
Southward the path descends to level terrain, grassy fields held captive by the
forest in the east and the watchful hills in the north, west and south. Farther
west, the Blue Mountains points its snowy fingers to the heavens.
name: Sloping Path

desc:
On this rocky height nothing grows but stubbled grass. The ground is strewn with
boulders and slabs of granite, huge rock relics unmoved since the ending of the
second age of the sun. The grinning streak of a ravine lies some way to the
north, while south the hill drops sharply down to lower slopes.
name: Path across a Tor

desc:
You stand on top of a hill, a good lookout point to the rural landscape
surrounding you. The forest lies dense to the northeast, east and southeast,
only broken by freckles of grassland. A strange sight in the lowlands to the
south is a circle of monolithic stones. No descent to the forest or the plains
is possible; you can only follow the path north or west through the hills.
name: Path Overlooking a Forest

desc:
You stand on a slope slowly ascending from the grasslands to the south. Up
north, the path climbs steeper into rugged hills, while south you see the path
curving to your left. You can also make your way east, along the sides of the
hills to the north.
name: Faint Path

desc:
The hillside ends abruptly at a tall rockface which tilts slightly out the top.
You spot several bird nests in small niches in the rock high above your head.
Looking directly south across the fields, you see some upturned slabs of rock
forming a circle; lowering your gaze you see two paths come together in the
fields below.
name: On a Hillside

desc:
You stand on a hillside some way above a plain. The ascending slopes to the
north are too steep for climbing, forcing you either to follow the angled
terrain west or retreat to a path down on the plain south of here. Looking east,
you see a line of marshy grassland almost totally obscured by the surrounding
forest.
name: On a Hillside

desc:
This east-west path trudges in the midst of wet-soiled grass, which in turn is
surrounded by dense forest to the north and south. Looking downhill to the
southwest, you see some plains in the distance, while east you see a tiny lake
by the path.
name: Track Bordering a Forest

desc:
Here the swampy terrain makes leaving the path a treacherous affair. A dry patch
of moss gives you a chance to go north into the woods, but following the path
east or west looks more inviting. North of east you see the muddy marsh envelop
a tiny lake.
name: Surrounded by Trees

desc:
At this point the path rounds the southeastern corner of a baby lake, ovally
shaped and not much larger than a pool. Some of its water is drained by a small
brook running southeast into the forest. There being no possible entry into the
forest here, your only choice is to follow the path north or west through the
marsh.
name: By a Lake

desc:
You are close to the northeastern shore of a very small lake. The ground is wet
and spongy, and on the opposite side of the lake it looks even worse. To get
there you must follow the path south, lest you wish to ascend the path northeast
to drier land. As for the forest lying in all other directions, one visible
entry point can be discerned to the north.
name: On the Shore of a Lake

desc:
Wandering on in the monotonous landscape of firs, you almost stumble into a
crevice in the undergrowth. It is only a narrow gap cutting north and south, and
you can see both of its terminations from where you stand. From somewhere comes
a faint whisper of running water, but around here the quietness is supreme
ruler.
name: Humid Forest

desc:
This place seems like a fortress of trees. Wherever you stand, some needled
branch is bound to touch your shoulder. You see nothing of your surroundings
except heavily branched fir trunks, but you should be able to press south or
east to get out of this place.
name: Trailside Forest

desc:
'For east or west all woods must fail...' goes an old traveller song, but it
might seem vain to tell the one who is lost in darkness about the beauty of
sunlight. And right here the forest seems to have no end, two dark walls
embracing the vein of grassland on which this east-west track runs.
name: Passage through the Trees

desc:
The small track crosses a small mound from which you can look down on an oval
basin to the southwest, which is centred in a strip of boggy grassland lying in
between the trees. You can enter the dark forest by going north or south, and
eastward the track leaves down through waves of tall grass.
name: Passage through the Trees

desc:
It seems entering the forest here was a waste of time. The trees stand closely
massed, forcing you to turn back in the same direction from whence you came, you
only gain being loose branches tangling into you clothing and equipment.
name: In the Forest

desc:
Blueberry bushes grow abundantly here, covering most of the soil between the
trees. A rubble occupies the south side of a nearby mound. Two small holes
between the rocks are probably fox-holes, or the dens of some other animal. The
way north is blocked by a dense shrubbery, but any other way will carry you on
through the forest.
name: In the Forest

desc:
Pine trees dominate your surroundings, and the ground is specked with needles
and cones. A squirrel notices you and scramble up the nearest tree, while a
roused chattering of birds spreads the message of your arrival. To the west
there seems to be an increasing growth of bushes and firs, and southwards the
forest dwindles.
name: In the Forest

desc:
The forest does not give you much space to work your way through, and in the
spaces between the trees bushes grow, some as tall as waist-high. Many times you
are forced to retrace your step and try some other route. The many birds in the
trees make quite a fuss over your passage.
name: In the Forest

desc:
You are dwarfed by these cloud-climbing giants among trees. These must be
descendants of the mighty brethil which once grew lavishly in the forests of
sunken Beleriand. A good climber would certainly be able to get a splendid view
of the forest and its surroundings from one of these tree-tops.
name: Majestic Trees

desc:
You stand on the borderline between birches to the north and firs to the south.
A thread of trampled undergrowth comes from the east and widens some- what as it
enters the birch forest to the north. At times you spot shadowy figures moving
at safe distance among the trees. To the southwest you see the tops of some
trees as tall and slender as castle spires.
name: Mixed Forest

desc:
You are deep within the forest. A plentitude of firs surround a group of slim
pines growing on an elevation in the terrain. The extreme density of the forest
leaves you clueless as to what lies ahead, but a barely visible strip of
downtrodden brush trails west.
name: Dense Forest

desc:
You are wandering between a few of Yavanna's innumerable creations. To the west
is an expanding section of birches, and southwards lie denser woods of conifers.
In the brush around your feet bloom fragrant flowers from some broad-leafed
herbs.
name: Mixed Forest

desc:
You are in a dead and silent part of the forest. No wind stirs, and it is just
as well, because there are no leaves to rustle, no pollen or spores to be
spread. Only the dust and smothering smell of dehydrated and worm-eaten old wood
can be carried on the breeze from here. Why this place has been cursed by a
dry-spell you cannot tell, for most of the forest around you is in contrast
luxuriously well-growing.
name: Dead Trees

desc:
You move on into the thick forest, and soon you find yourself among maple
shrubs. You cannot on your life figure out where you came from, but westish
looks like the most comfortable going from here. You can spy the crest of a hill
over the crowns of trees to the north.
name: Dense Shrubs

desc:
North of you stands a cliff, its lower parts overgrown by the forest vege-
tation, and to the west a group of stooping trees cling to the rockface. The
ground is dry and dusty in place, more so to the south, where a stand of black
trees can be seen.
name: Light Forest under the Cliff

desc:
The musty odour of rotten vegetation fills your nostrils. Decaying boughs and
branches cover the emaciated soil. The remaining trees are just naked, blackish
trunks with peeling bark or no bark at all. To the west you see more
discomforting forest death, but anywhere else will take you to fresher parts of
the forest.
name: Dead Trees

desc:
Many different sorts of trees are gathered around you. The greyish trunks of
pines are the most prominent. Two young birches stand leaning against each
other, and the occasional ash tree can also be spotted. Looking northeast, you
see an immense oak tree, and to the south the forest opens up onto a path.
name: In the Forest

desc:
Here grows a lonely contingent of fragrant shrubs carrying white and blue
flowers. All around it the thick forest dwells, mostly firs, but also some
dry-barked pines with their few branches set high up on tall trunks. To the east
the trees stand further apart than north and west. You decide it to be the
easiest way out of the forest.
name: Forest Thicket

desc:
This place in the forest stands out for its ordinarity. Surrounded by a mass of
trees which all looks exactly the same, you have completely lost all directional
sense. You decide you can either use the sun and the moon as beacons, or just
wander randomly on.
name: Brooding Forest

desc:
This part of the forest is covered in vegetation which makes it very difficult
for you to tell the direction you are heading. Most of the sun's light is
eliminated, due to the entanglement of greenery far above. Some natural light
slips in however, whether it be the sun or moon, and from this light you can see
that there is only more forest all around you.
name: Entangled Forest

desc:
Much to your relief you can make out a path to the south. The dense greenery and
tangled foliage make orienting almost impossible. Every turn looks the same and
the trees obscure any tell-tale landmarks. The air smells heavily of rot, as the
shade of the ground keeps the bacteria in constant supply. A feast for the tiny
creatures, the deadfall from the trees is quickly decomposed.
name: Entangled Forest

desc:
A little trickle of water filters into a pool bordering some rocks. A couple of
fish dart around it, and you notice stepping stones placed evenly across the
pond. A small dock lies to the south, accessible easily by the stones. Flowers
of different colors grace the banks of the water, and birds sing in happy
harmony. A high hedge to the east prevents you from going in that direction.
name: A Small Pond

desc:
This dock appears to see little use. The familiar sounds of birds singing and
the rustling of animals through the underbrush are curiously absent. A line of
stepping stones extend from the dock across a small pond to the north, while the
Nan Duin flows strongly past to the south. The only sign of habitation nearby is
a ramshackle cabin to the east, which may house the builder of this dock.
name: On a Dock

desc:
You find yourself in the heart of a light forest. The trees and undergrowth are
not dense, and you can move around easily. Many flowers grow here, where the Nan
Duin crosses the forest. You can see some squirrels on the treetops, and you
hear birds tweeting.
name: Narbrethil Forest

desc:
The huge trees overwhelm you as the diameter alone is bigger than the fattest
troll in Arda. Their enormous branches make ideal homes for the local animal
population. Squirrels scamper around the limbs, while a woodpecker concentrates
on the trunk. The tree-tops are high, their leaves blocking out much of the
natural light from above. In all directions you can see only more trees and
dense vegetation.
name: Narbrethil Forest

desc:
The huge trees of the Narbrethil Forest surround you in every direction. Their
mighty roots delve deep into the earth. Small, colorful birds sing merrily all
around, making you feel relaxed. An medium sized anthill stands near one of the
trees.
name: Narbrethil Forest

desc:
You are crossing a beautiful forest north of the Nan Duin. The trees must be
very old, since their size is truly impressive. All around you, the rustling of
the leaves and the birds chirping make you feel at ease. You can see a small
hole on one of the trees, it is probably a squirrel's home.
name: Narbrethil Forest

desc:
You find yourself in the heart of a light forest, on the northern side of the
Nan Duin. Small birds are chirping amid the rustling leaves of the huge trees. A
rivulet of warm water flows from the north towards the river. As you continue
your journey across the greenery, you relax in this peaceful environment.
name: Narbrethil Forest

desc:
You find yourself in a light forest on the northern bank of the Nan Duin. The
undergrowth is not at all entangled, and you can walk around with ease. Here and
there, small yellow flowers and red berries grow among the huge trees. Their
sweet smell fills your nostrils.
name: Narbrethil Forest

desc:
Worn steps, which the Dwarves carved into the relatively soft rock of the river
bank, lead south and west to the calm water of the river. At the top of the
steps, a small, seldom used trail begins its long journey towards the Blue
Mountains to the north. Beyond the river, which enters a forest of huge trees
not too far away, you spot a high stone tower. It probably was built to guard
these lands against invaders and other dangers.
name: Bank of the Nan Duin

desc:
In a clearing of the Narbrethil Forest, fuming water flows from a crack in the
ground and fills a small pool before finding its way to the river. The heat
locks this place in an eternal summer. Bunches of small wild flowers grow around
the spring; a deer, which was grazing here, flees silently into the woods as
soon as you arrive.
name: Hot Spring

desc:
You are crossing a beautiful forest on the slopes of the Blue Mountains. The
trees must be very old, since their size is truly impressive. All around you,
the rustling of the leaves and the birds chirping make you feel at ease. A light
breeze from the east carries the scent of flowers.
name: Narbrethil Forest

desc:
The huge trees of the forest far to the east could not grow here as well, due to
the thick layer of stones above the soil. A few long striped snakes dart here
and there between the rocks, in search of food.
name: Rocky Plains

desc:
You are in the middle of a rocky plain, almost devoid of vegetation; a few
patches of wild flowers, which somehow managed to find water below the thick
layer of stones, are the only exception.
name: Rocky Plains

desc:
You are at the edge of a rocky plain, nearly devoid of vegetation. Only in the
cracks of the large stones some moss has managed to grow. A few striped snakes
are slithering between the rocks. To the south the plains are flanked by the Nan
Duin.
name: Rocky Plains

desc:
You are in the midst of a lightly wooded area. The woods are mainly composed of
young pine trees, barely larger than saplings. Passage to the north is blocked
by a steep rock face, which continues to the west. As the ground rises to the
east, the trees thin and bare patches of stony terrain are visible. The woods
extend downhill to the south, where the pines are gradually supplanted by larger
and more numerous oaks.
name: Climbing Woods

desc:
There are few trees here under this sharp cliffwall bordering the forest. You
can follow it west or head south towards a great oak which stands out among the
lesser trees. The cliff is smooth with no obvious projections, so climbing it is
clearly out of the question.
name: Sheltered by a Cliff

desc:
This steep slope is covered with bushes and small trees. Below you, to the
north, there seems to be an entrance to a mine. The ground before it is grey and
covered with pebbles. Further north the valley of the Nan Duin is meandering
through the landscape like a giant snake.
name: Above a Mine Entrance

desc:
The mountain slopes upwards to your north, small crooked birches cover the area,
but below you the vegetation is flourishing. The hills that stretch out to the
south seem to be a perfect place for shy animals to hide in.
name: Woody Slope

desc:
You found a narrow tunnel leading down into the darkness. The granite walls are
covered with a layer of encrusted smoke particles, and the air is very thick.
The echo returns to you each noise that you make, amplified and distorted in a
malignant way.
name: Forgotten Tunnel

desc:
The tunnel here branches in a Y junction. To the south, this dreadful place is
illuminated by a pale reddish glow. The tunnel also continues to the east, where
it descends slowly, and north to the exit. Sudden gusts of wind bring a faint
smell of brimstone to your nostrils.
name: Forgotten Tunnel

desc:
This part of the tunnel is hotter than the rest, and bathed in a reddish glow
coming from a gaping hole in the floor. The air is difficult to breathe, as the
smell of smoke and brimstone is overpowering. The walls here are even more
blackened and scorched than elsewhere in the tunnel. The cavern steeply slopes
down both to the east and to the north.
name: Forgotten Tunnel

desc:
You are standing in a narrow, dark tunnel. Its granite walls seem to have been
blackened in some sort of conflagration. The atmosphere is thick, and a feeling
of dread pervades the place. On the southern wall, a sort of shaft leads up. The
walls of the shaft are extremely hard, and as smooth as glass, making the ascent
impossible. The terrain ascends to the west, where, in the distance, you can see
a pale reddish glow.
name: Forgotten Tunnel

desc:
You are standing in a narrow, dark tunnel. Its granite walls seem to have been
blackened in some sort of conflagration. The atmosphere is thick, and a feeling
of dread pervades the place.
name: Forgotten Tunnel

desc:
You barely manage to squeeze yourself through a narrow crack in the tunnel and
you reach a dark, cold, damp room. Red-stained water drips on you from the
ceiling and collects into a small pool at your feet. A pile of old bones lies on
the bottom of the pool.
name: Dark Chamber

desc:
You find yourself at the western edge of a dense, gloomy forest. Beyond the Nan
Duin, which flows calmly north of here, you can barely see a path which winds
its way far to the north. The trees are less dense to the west. East of here,
however, the forest becomes VERY dark and dangerous. Ominous howls and cries
filter from there; and the growth seems so tangled that it must be very
difficult to find one's way.
name: Dense Forest

desc:
The thick stone walls of this tower rise high all around you. The windows are
very narrow, and the room is lit by a huge fireplace. Some hewn wood has been
stacked near the fire. Opposite the entrance, a spiral staircase leads up.
name: Dwarven Guard Tower

desc:
You have reached the flat roof of the high Dwarven tower. A long spiral
staircase leads inside. From here you can see the valley of the Nan Duin all
around. The calm, clear river flows from the western mountains and it enters a
large forest far to the east. A long, worn, winding trail descends from the Blue
Mountains southwest; it reaches the foot of this tower and turns to the
southeast, where it enters the forest. Another trail, leading north, begins on
the other bank of Nan Duin, being flanked by barren plains on one side and by a
light forest on the other. West of here there are many rough hills, which have
been extensively mined by the Dwarves, while to the south you can see a dark,
gloomy forest. This is an excellent place to guard the lands of the Dwarves: as
you can realise, any movement on the river or the trail would be spotted
quickly.
name: Top of the Tower

desc:
This building houses the mounts that travellers to the Ered Luin need for
negotiating the miles. In the main, ponies fill the stalls: the favoured mount
of the dwarves. However, larger mounts are also catered for, as well as exotic
pets. A set of shuttered windows to the northern end of the building show a good
view of the nearby Nan Duin.
name: The Stables

desc:
You have entered a groove in the forest undergrowth. Its slippery sides are
about ten feet high, but luckily the southern edge slopes gently up to the
forest proper. The bottom is layered with mushy moss, soaked by an artery of
underground water surfacing for the length of this cut in the forest floor.
name: Dank Grove

desc:
You had to climb quite high to get a good view, and standing with your feet
cautiously balanced on one of the highest branches, you feel a slight swaying
caused by your weight and the wind. Still, an excellent view of the lands of
Lindon presents itself before your eyes.
name: In a Tree-top

desc:
You enter a dark, moist cave about fifteen feet across. Rust-coloured water
trickles down the far wall, forming a pool of unclean water covering half of the
