cave's floor. You can make out no other exit than the cramped tunnel through
which you came.
name: Hollow

desc:
The land slowly rises to the northeast. Trees cover the slope, making travel
torturous. However unlikely, the trees seem to grow even more closely together
to the west. A tall oak tree dominates the others. Its branches reach out far
above the tops of all the other trees. The wooded area succumbs to the skies
towards the south.
name: Forested Slope

desc:
The forest thins here somewhat, allowing a patch of larch-wood to flourish. The
wooded area allows easier passage than the dense forests closing in on the
larch-wood. The local trees protect a varied growth of bushes and flowers from
the mountains far to the north. The forest grows wilder and more entangled to
the west. The forest continues up the rising hills to the north. Trees grow
sparsely upon the plains further to the east.
name: Patch of Larch-Wood

desc:
You are in the forest, south of a huge oak and north of a thin forest path. A
group of silver-grey ash trees have taken root here, some of the wearing
clusters of green flowers. To the east and west the forest seems green and rich,
but northwesterly it looks dark and squalid.
name: In the Forest

desc:
You are standing beneath the lower boughs of a sublime oak tree. Overhead a
swallow rides the wind, maybe it is protecting nestlings in the tree-top. An
acorn falls to the ground, probably because of the harsh rapping of a wood-
pecker. To the north you see the great wall of a cliff causing a sharp ending to
the forest.
name: Beneath a Large Oak

desc:
In these unhappy days colonies of Dwarves are spread far across Middle-earth,
and Dwarves must sometimes travel far from their homes. The walls are covered in
sacks and bags, fishing rods and tackles, and everything else that a travelling
Dwarf might require on the road.
name: Althof's Trade Supply Shop

desc:
Tall ornamented pillars rise towards the smooth curved ceiling where small round
holes provide the inn with fresh air and light during the days. Around the
walls, small booths have been excavated, containing tables with surrounding
chairs for the guests. A large fireplace is placed in the centre of the room,
and a big counter can be seen at the far end of it. Behind the counter a narrow
corridor leads to the inn-rooms.
name: Vubur's Inn

desc:
A large archway, made entirely of marble, leads into this home. An example of
the finest Dwarven craftsmanship, the stone is unblemished, save for an
inscription at the top and each base of the arch, stating in both Khuzdul and
Westron that this is the home of Durin's Army. Near the bottom of the archway,
smaller inscriptions can be seen.
name: Marble Archway

desc:
You have entered a dense, gloomy, entangled forest. The trees grow in all
directions, and the thick undergrowth forces you to turn frequently, totally
ruining your sense of direction. From time to time, a couple of red eyes blink
in the dark, and you hear deep growls and high pitched howls. The entwined
branches of the trees above you are so thick that almost no light filters to the
ground.
name: Dense Forest

desc:
A gravel path winds around the top of a small promontory jutting out of the side
of the mountain. The park itself is unimpressive; several sullen trees stand
atop a mangy patch of grass. The surroundings, however, are magnificent. The
Blue Mountains tower up just to the west. From your vantage several hundred feet
above the ground you can see quite a ways to the east, where the Nan Duin winds
through the plains on its way to the Nabrethril Forest.
name: A Small Park

desc:
This large hallway is carved right into the side of the mountain. Hung on the
sides of this hallway are large colourful murals, interspaced between torches
which serve to light it. A large stone door is set in the north wall, which
opens up into a small park. To the south, two wooden doubledoors are set in
place to partition it off from this area.
name: A Stone Hallway

desc:
The large stone hallway to the north leads into this room. Carved out of solid
rock, this dome shaped room has many mystical signs and symbols carved onto its
walls. Large benches surround a stone altar which has two large fire pits on
either side. A large circle of green tiles has been set onto the floor which
surrounds the altar and benches. Another tile pattern on the altar is the shape
of a fist.
name: A Stone Altar

desc:
This hallway was cut straight from solid rock. The ceiling and walls are coarse
and lack refinement. Along the walls are mounted brackets containing lit torches
that cast dancing shadows down the length of the hallway. There is a large
opening to the south from which a great deal of heat and the sound of hammer
crashing against an anvil emerge. To the west another opening can be seen
covered by a woolen curtain.
name: Crude Hallway

desc:
Here, far below the surface, the water is cold. In the vague light from above
the green and brown vegetation can be seen on the muddy bottom, gently waving as
the water slowly moves back and forth. It is extremely difficult to see anything
further than fifteen feet away.
name: At the Bottom of a Lake

desc:
The walls of this stone entryway were carved long ago by the hands of superior
Dwarven engineers. The walls and floor have a sturdy look, and the stone
comprising the ceiling contains intricate carvings of battle scenes.
name: Stone Hallway

desc:
This lengthy stone hallway shows the remarkable skill of its builders. In
addition to the intricately-carved and gem-laden floor, walls and ceilings,
numerous statues are positioned throughout the lengthy hall. A closer
examination of the statues reveals that they celebrate the heroic deeds of
legendary dwarves.
name: Hall of Heroes

desc:
A massive arched ceiling traverses the entire length of this long, stone
hallway. Doric columns run the distance of the passage and help support the
heavy ceiling. Underneath foot, carved tiles possess elaborate designs. A
gigantic marble statue standing in the centre of the hall depicts a dwarf
holding a gigantic weapon. To the south lies a similar stone chamber while to
the north a huge table is barely visible.
name: Hall of Heroes

desc:
This magnificent stone hall extends from above and below. Although the walls are
solid granite, they were carved and polished brilliantly by master craftsmen.
While the room has many small figurines, one larger statue honouring the god
Thoiram is the focal point of this area.
name: Hall of Heroes

desc:
In contrast to the otherwise clean rooms in this home, this place is a mess.
Weapons and armour hang randomly on the walls, without thought for order or
property. Axes and hammers dominate the ranks of weapons, as they seem to be the
inhabitant's weapon of choice. On the right wall, you see old breastplates and
other pieces of beaten-up armour hanging. They were probably used in battles
long ago.
name: Tror's Barracks

desc:
This broad tunnel delves slowly down into the great Blue Mountains. It ends to
the east at the base of a wide staircase, leading to the upper, public areas of
the caverns. The corridor is broad and tall and brightly lit by many lanterns
along the walls.
name: Broad Tunnel

desc:
This broad staircase connects the lower levels of these halls with the topmost
level opening on the outside world. Dwarves ascend and descend these steps,
buying provisions at the stores above, and venturing out into the forests and
meadows of these mountain vales, or returning from abroad to their homes. The
risers are inscribed at the edges with intricately detailed abstract patterns.
name: Marble Staircase

desc:
This large room is wide enough for an enormous wooden table and a dozen or so
chairs. The table itself is piled with papers, which appear to be mainly maps
and city plans for new excavations and mining. A small shelf stands beside the
table with rolls of paper neatly tucked away. Most likely the plans, plats and
copies of deeds of finished homes currently in use.
name: Deeds and Digging Office

desc:
Although this room is lit rather poorly, the jovial atmosphere in the room
reveals that the darkness is not indicative of danger. This tavern seems to be
an ideal place for dwarves to meet and socialise, while resting up from long
journeys with a refreshing brew.
name: The Battalion Tavern

desc:
In this echoing dome lit by the light of crystal lamps, bright gemstones and
veins of precious ores can be seen. The marble walls are jet black and polished
like glass. Intricately carved forms within the walls depict a scene of battle
from days long ago. A sturdy weapons rack laden with axes of various shapes and
sizes stands in the corner surrounded on all sides by bright banners, fine
armour and jewelled weapons which adorn the walls.
name: Slidar's Barracks

desc:
The hall bends sharply here, curving north and west. All around you are
entrances into simple homes, carved into the chamber's walls. Each wooden door
fits snugly into its stone frame, with straight sides and a curved top, and the
name of the home's owner carved proudly in the centre.
name: Dum Khazdul

desc:
Here the hall bends sharply to the south and west. Along most of the way are
Dwarven-homes dug into the walls. To the north, though, there is no wall, only
an opening into an enormous cavern.
name: Dum Khazdul

desc:
Various Dwarven homes fill the southern wall of this passage, and around many
doorsteps stand small groups of Dwarves, engaged in conversation and greeting
passersby. There is no wall to the north, only an opening into a large cavern.
name: Dum Khazdul

desc:
To the west one can see a small wooden structure, where the guards loyal to the
Emissary of Durin's House can take turns to rest. The well-worn stone floor
leads to a roomy hall to the north. To the south the bulky metal-plated wooden
gate protects the entrance.
name: The Entrance of the Emissary of Durin's House

desc:
This hall is one of the greatest achievements of modern masonry, perhaps not
comparable to the great works of Khazad-dum, but still magnificent. In the
centre an old table with 24 chairs around it waits to host the next feast. Shiny
metal in the ceiling together with the numerous torches create the illusion of
stars in the sky.
name: The Great Hall

desc:
This looks like a fine place to end the hard day of a military leader and start
a well deserved rest. A door is complemented with woolen cloth to make it
soundproof. A simple but comfortable bed waits for its master.
name: The Emissary's Retreat

desc:
Following the small stream that flows from the pond, a traveller would find
themselves in the entrance of this large cave. Several torches sit in a metal
bracket on the wall, drenched in oil and producing a blazing fire. Dominating
the scene is a rack which stands beside the wall with several pegs hanging upon
it. This arrangement is home to a great many various type of weapons and armour.
name: Ippo's Barrack

desc:
This small room looks more like a tiny cave. There are no distinctive walls or a
ceiling. A small, seldom used firepit with a spit over it is cut into the floor
in the middle of the room. A small pile of trash has accumulated beside the
firepit. There is a small pallet in the corner where one might sleep. The room
is lit by a solitary torch that hangs aside the firepit, casting a dim glow
throughout the room.
name: A Small Bedchamber

desc:
This crude room is filled with the ringing of hammer and anvil. Waves of heat
spread from the furnace cut into the western face of the room. A small cart
filled with bars of iron rests in the corner.
name: Smithy

desc:
Here, bookshelves adorn every inch of wall except for the oakdoor leading to the
south. Books lie open and lay about everywhere gathering dust. A small windowed
hole in the ceiling provides a hint of what lies outside in the mountainous land
of the Blue Mountains.
name: Small, Cramped Library

desc:
Lit torches make shadows dance along the walls in this curving hallway, which
stretches out to the north and east. The polished walls are completely smooth to
the touch. Small veins of ore can be seen, illuminating the corridor with a
faint glow.
name: Curving Passageway

desc:
The large hallway curves slowly to the west and south here, barely lit by the
ensconced torches on the walls. Small veins of ore glow slightly in the dim
light of the torches. The ceiling is quite high, echoing any sound of movement
in the otherwise still air.
name: Curving Passageway

desc:
The high ceiling of the corridor rises even higher, opening into an enormous
hall. A fireplace crackles in the centre of the room, filling the hall with
warmth. Comfortable chairs have been placed around the fireplace, allowing the
residents of this place to relax after a hard day. Small corridors lead off to
the north and south.
name: Durin's Hall

desc:
A small, cramped chamber opens up from the corridor to the north. This room is
large enough for only one inhabitant to sleep in comfortably, though more could
occupy the area in time of need. A statue in the likeness of Durin the Deathless
stands is the corner, a monument to the great Dwarf-lord.
name: Cramped Retreat

desc:
The walls are lined with Dwarven historical artefacts, setting this place of
worship apart from the other temples. On the western wall hangs a portrait of
Durin the Deathless himself, standing in all his glory. In keeping with the vow
of poverty chosen by the Patriarch, the room lacks many decorations and is
poorly-lit, giving the Dwarves that pray here a sense of unity as they pray to
their deity.
name: Prayer Room

desc:
Inside the sleeping quarters of the Bodyguard of Durin's Army can be found the
tools of his trade. Glittering armour both new and old lines the walls, waiting
to turn the blade of a fell minion of Sauron's. Swords, axes and bows rest in
weapon racks, biding their time until they are called forth to do battle with an
evil Orc or Troll.
name: Stone Chamber

desc:
The large hall stretches out deep into the mountains. A strong but pleasant
smell of food wafts into the room from the south, where the sounds of cooking
and cleaning can often be heard. Several large tables have been placed around
the hall, apparently made for a large number of Dwarves to feast at.
name: Durin's Hall

desc:
This large kitchen is roomy, allowing many Dwarves to create a large feast for a
seemingly even larger number of Dwarves waiting outside. Since Dwarves are
generally very fond of food and drink, there are large pantries leading away
from the kitchen, where any number of delicacies can always be found.
name: Large Kitchen

desc:
This great hall, unlike others, is not a monument to heros, nor a testimony to
the greatness of kings long buried. It is a hall for all Dwarves, a great cavern
dedicated to the honor of every Dwarf, great or meek, living or dead. The avenue
is lined with trees of stone, trees from whose boughs hang flowers or fruit
carved from fine opals, sapphires, diamonds, and other jewels. The walls are not
covered in reliefs, nor molded by carvings. In fact, they seem utterly bare
until you look closer. Slowly your eyes focus on the marks impressed upon the
stone: names. The name of every Dwarf living and dead is here, to be honored by
all, and remembered.
name: Felak Khazad

desc:
A small, seldom used trail leads north, towards the Blue Mountains, and south,
where it ends on the bank of a river. The trail follows the edge of a light
forest, where you can hear birds tweeting. It should be safe to relax and rest
your weary feet in the shade of the trees, it looks like a very peaceful place.
Beyond the river, a tower dominates the landscape.
name: Trail in the Blue Mountains

desc:
This place in the forest stands out for its ordinarity. Surrounded by a mass of
trees which all looks exactly the same, you have completely lost all directional
sense. You decide you can either use the sun and the moon as beacons, or just
wander randomly on.
name: Brooding Forest

desc:
Decorative weapons and a great many torches fill up the rooms walls while padded
mats cover the floor. There are blood stains along the walls and on the floors
looking almost as if a battle has been waged here.
name: Combat Training Room

desc:
This room barely fits the bed and wash basin that lie here. The covers of the
bed are the roughest of wool and lie in a jumbled mess at the end of the bed.
One torch burns softly above the doorway providing enough light to fill the
small bed-chamber.
name: Cramped Bedroom

desc:
This is a smaller room than the one north, filled with large bookshelves, which
are cluttered with scrolls and books. Upon a table in the centre of this room
lie several large tomes. At the table stands a very large comfortable chair. At
one end of this room a stone bed where the master of this temple sleeps.
name: The Cloister

desc:
Down and down you slide, along the glassy walls of this tunnel, away from the
lava and the billowing fumes...
name: Slippery Shaft
